description: A request for computational work. Published by the requester in their own repo before the work runs, so the provider has something to commit a receipt against.

model.description: Opaque model identifier the requester wants the provider to honor.

inputCommitment.description: SHA-256 hex over the SEALED input plaintext bytes the provider decrypts — i.e. the exact bytes both the requester (before sealing) and the provider (after opening) hash, so it is self-consistent without either side parsing the payload. The plaintext is never on-chain. The byte layout is described by `inputFormat`: absent/`text` means the bytes are the raw prompt string; `messages-v1` means they are the UTF-8 of the canonical multimodal envelope (see `inputFormat`).

inputFormat.description: How to interpret the sealed input bytes that `inputCommitment` covers. Absent (legacy) or `text`: the bytes are the raw prompt string. `messages-v1`: the bytes are the UTF-8 of a canonical-JSON multimodal envelope { v: 1, messages: [ { role, content } ] }, where `content` is either a string or an array of parts — { type: "text", text } and { type: "image", mime, data } with `data` base64-encoded image bytes inline. A verifier reconstructs the same canonical bytes to recompute `inputCommitment`. Additive: providers that don't understand a format reject the job rather than mis-serving it.

inputCipherURL.description: Optional URL where the encrypted prompt blob can be fetched. May be a PDS blob (at://...) or any other content-addressed URL. The commitment is what matters; this is a convenience pointer.

priceCeiling.description: Maximum price the requester will accept for this job. The provider's receipt MUST list a price <= this ceiling, else the receipt is invalid.

acceptedProviders.description: Specific provider DIDs allowed to serve this job. Empty/absent means any provider that meets acceptedTrustLevel.

acceptedTrustLevel.description: Minimum trust level the requester will accept from a provider serving this job.

acceptedExchanges.description: Exchange DIDs allowed to settle this job. MUST be non-empty in practice; the named paymentAuthorization MUST cover one of these.

paymentAuthorization.description: Strong-ref to a dev.cocore.compute.paymentAuthorization record in the same repo, naming an exchange and a ceiling >= this job's priceCeiling.

expiresAt.description: Provider must complete by this time. Receipts whose completedAt > expiresAt are invalid.

outputSchema.description: Optional JSON Schema constraining the model's output. When present, the provider MUST pass it to the inference engine as a response_format guided-decoding constraint so the output conforms. The schema is public (not encrypted) — it describes the output shape, not the input prompt. A receipt's generationParams.outputSchemaHash MUST match SHA-256 of the canonical JSON of this field. Additive: providers that don't understand structured generation reject the job rather than serving unconstrained output.

strict.description: Whether to enforce strict schema adherence. When true, the engine must guarantee the output validates against the schema.

schema.description: The actual JSON Schema object that constrains the model output.

tools.description: Optional list of tool/function definitions the model may call. Public (not encrypted) — describes available functions, not user data. A receipt's generationParams.toolSchemaHash MUST match SHA-256 of the canonical JSON of this field. Additive: providers that don't support tool calling reject the job rather than ignoring the tools.

type.description: The tool type. Currently only 'function' is supported.

name.description: The function name the model calls to invoke this tool.

description.description: What the function does, so the model knows when to call it.

parameters.description: JSON Schema describing the function's arguments.

toolChoice.description: Optional tool choice strategy. 'auto' (default) lets the model decide whether to call a tool; 'none' disables tool calling; 'required' forces at least one tool call. Additive — absent means 'auto'.

toolChoiceFunction.description: When toolChoice is 'required', optionally force the model to call this specific function (by name). Maps to the OpenAI tool_choice object form { type: 'function', function: { name } }. Additive — absent means any tool may be called.
